title: 3 Hotel REITs Poised to Benefit from the World Cup
author: Chris Markoch
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is expected to generate over $2.4 billion in incremental economic value across U.S., Canada, and Mexico accommodations, with 21.3 million hotel room nights projected. Three hotel REITs—Host Hotels & Resorts, Park Hotels & Resorts, and Ryman Hospitality Properties—are positioned to benefit from World Cup demand. All three stocks have surged significantly this year but are showing technical signs of momentum cooling, with analysts suggesting patient investors may wait for pullbacks before adding exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Up 40% in 2026 with concrete World Cup tie-in (Fairmont Mayakoba selected to house national teams). Management raised full-year guidance citing World Cup demand. Most technically healthy chart with steady uptrend and bullish MACD, though trading above consensus price target suggests gains may be priced in.

Up 30% from May lows with direct exposure to urban World Cup host cities through Hilton spinoff portfolio. Strong parabolic move in late May/early June, though trading well above consensus price target of $12.68. MACD histogram shrinking suggests momentum may be cooling.

Gaylord Hotels brand positioned in high-demand World Cup markets including Dallas (hosting semifinal). Steady uptrend since April low with cleanest chart of the three. Carries consensus Buy rating, though trading near consensus price target. MACD histogram flattening suggests momentum cooling after strong run.

title: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. Declares Second Quarter Dividend
author: Globe Newswire
description: Ryman Hospitality Properties (NYSE: RHP) announced a second quarter cash dividend of $1.20 per share, payable on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of June 30, 2026. The company is a leading lodging and hospitality REIT specializing in upscale convention center resorts and entertainment experiences.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a substantial quarterly dividend of $1.20 per share, demonstrating financial strength and confidence in cash generation. Dividend declarations are typically viewed positively as they indicate profitability and shareholder-friendly capital allocation policies.
Marriott is mentioned only as the management company for RHP's hotel portfolio. The article provides no information about Marriott's performance or strategic implications, so sentiment is neutral.

title: A $39 Billion Empire and a 5% Dividend From Nashville's Front Porch
author: Bryan White
description: The article compares two live entertainment investment opportunities: Live Nation Entertainment, which dominates the U.S. concert industry with 80% control of primary ticketing and 460 venues globally, and Ryman Hospitality Properties, a hotel REIT offering a 5% dividend yield while owning iconic Nashville venues like the Grand Ole Opry and Ryman Auditorium. Both companies are investing in hard-to-replicate physical assets positioned to profit from growing demand for live entertainment.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Dominates the U.S. concert industry with ~80% control of primary ticketing, operates 460 venues globally (tripled since 2020), captures majority of fan spending, and has guided for double-digit adjusted operating income growth in 2026 with strong sponsorship deal bookings.

Offers attractive 5% dividend yield, owns iconic irreplaceable assets (Grand Ole Opry, Ryman Auditorium), operates five of the 10 largest nongaming convention hotels in the U.S., has recovered from pandemic with AFFO and dividends up 23-29% from 2019 levels, and benefits from predictable convention business with years-in-advance bookings.

title: 3 Dividend Stocks to Buy Right Now for Income and Upside
author: Bryan White
description: The article recommends three dividend stocks offering attractive yields and growth potential to compete with the 4.2% Treasury yield. UnitedHealth Group trades at a discount following earnings disappointment but maintains a safe 3.2% dividend with strong free cash flow. Ryman Hospitality Properties, a REIT owning major convention hotels and Nashville music venues, offers a 4.8% yield with solid booking growth. ONEOK, a diversified midstream energy company, provides a 5.1% yield and trades at a reasonable valuation despite recent gains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed outlook: Recent 20% stock decline due to rising medical care ratios and disappointing Medicare rate increases creates uncertainty. However, the 3.2% dividend is well-covered with $16 billion in free cash flow, and the stock trades at an attractive 15.5x forward earnings with expected 8.5% EPS growth.

Temporary headwinds from renovations and cancellations are offset by strong fundamentals: bookings up 8% for the year, 4.8% yield backed by a conservative 57% payout ratio, and compelling 12x AFFO valuation. Unique exposure to growing Nashville music scene provides upside potential.

Strong operational performance with 37% EBITDA growth driven by recent major acquisitions. Despite 15% recent gains, stock trades at attractive 11x EBITDA with 5.1% yield. Free cash flow payout ratio expected to improve as integration projects complete, supporting dividend sustainability and growth.

title: Ryman Hospitality Properties, Inc. Announces 2025 Tax Classification of Shareholder Dividend Distributions
author: Globe Newswire
description: Ryman Hospitality Properties (NYSE: RHP) announced the 2025 tax classification of its dividend distributions to shareholders. The company paid total dividends of $5.80 per share throughout 2025, with most distributions classified as ordinary non-qualifying dividends. A portion of the final dividend paid in January 2026 is classified as a return of capital, taxable in 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine tax classification disclosure required for REITs and does not contain information indicating positive or negative business developments. It is a standard administrative communication to shareholders about dividend tax treatment, with no material business impact implied.
